mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159398 thousand as of May 2013 reflects post-recession recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment trend
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, monetary tightening reduces equity valuations by raising discount rates and constraining credit availability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 319.615 as of May 2013
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or Rank IC of the interaction signal is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,400 thousand units signal strong residential construction demand that cascades into durable goods orders, employment, and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1501.0 thousand as of April 2013, above pre-crisis trend
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery with positive momentum
failure_condition: HOUST levels above 1400 do not produce significantly higher forward returns than levels below 1200 (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, signaling demand weakness and capacity underutilization that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.2062 and UNRATE at 4.2% as of April 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with labor market softening
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term has zero or positive correlation with forward returns over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1,400 thousand, indicating broad-based consumer and residential demand that supports earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 722708.0 million and HOUST at 1501.0 thousand as of April 2013
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of occurrences, or Rank IC of the joint signal is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases credit availability for corporate investment and M&A, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp over 3 months AND FEDFUNDS < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of May 2013, well above easing threshold
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with declining rates
failure_condition: Easing periods (FEDFUNDS < 2.0%) do not produce significantly higher 60-day returns than tightening periods (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC is negative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 persons per month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS monthly change < 100000
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% and PAYEMS at 159398 thousand as of May 2013, both showing strength
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising unemployment and slowing job creation
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction signal shows zero correlation with forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while remaining below 110 index points, signaling moderate supply-side expansion that supports profit margins without triggering inflation concerns, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.2062 as of April 2013, within moderate expansion range
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO growth > 0.4% do not produce significantly higher 5-day returns than months with slower growth (t-test p > 0.15), or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year window.
